title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Runtime Contract: Tenant Isolation Abuse
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must verify server identity, roots, client credentials, vhost permissions, rotation, redaction, and tenant abuse boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tenant isolation abuse increment in a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract. Learners pro…

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Tenant Isolation Abuse · Theory Tenant Isolation Abuse
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4775 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; reject stale connection and channel generations; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
